preamble: Similar to its shift to [æ], the pharyngeal fricative [ʕ] (Ayin) can shift to the back unrounded vowel [ʌ] (the caret or 'strut' vowel) when borrowed into English. This occurs when the pharyngeal constriction is perceived as 'darker' or when it coincides with a stressed, short syllable.

examples.note: The pharyngeal + back vowel sequence is often adapted into English as the stressed [ʌ] sound.
The short 'a' following Ayin is sometimes centralized/backed to [ʌ] in English pronunciation.
